node_types.description: A psychological state or mode (e.g., Vulnerable Child, Maladaptive Coping) relevant to schema therapy conceptualization and intervention.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category of mode (e.g., Child, Parent, Coping, Healthy)

properties.name: associated_needs
properties.description: Core needs linked to this mode

supported_by_chapters: 3 The Integrated Schema Therapy Treatment Program

node_types.description: A persistent, dysfunctional pattern of thoughts, feelings, and behaviors originating in childhood, as defined in schema therapy.

properties.name: schema_name
properties.description: Name of the early maladaptive schema

properties.name: content_area
properties.description: Content area grouping (e.g., Disconnection, Impaired Autonomy)

properties.name: associated_modes
properties.description: Schema modes commonly linked to this schema

properties.name: core_needs_unmet
properties.description: Core childhood needs unmet by this schema

node_types.description: A fundamental psychological need (e.g., secure attachment, autonomy) whose fulfillment or lack thereof shapes schema development.

properties.name: developmental_relevance
properties.description: Relevance to childhood development and schema formation

node_types.description: A structured schema therapy program, including format, duration, and session types.

node_types.description: A single therapy session, with details on type, focus, and interventions used.

properties.name: session_type
properties.description: Type of session (e.g., education, mode awareness, experiential)

properties.name: mode_focus
properties.description: Schema mode(s) targeted in the session

node_types.description: A specific therapeutic technique or strategy used in schema therapy.

properties.name: applicable_modes

node_types.description: A standardized tool or measure used for assessment in schema therapy (e.g., Schema Mode Inventory).

node_types.description: The clinical context in which schema therapy is delivered (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, day hospital).

node_types.description: Cultural or religious factors relevant to schema therapy, including Islamic-cultural alignment.

edge_types.description: Indicates that a patient exhibits or is conceptualized as having a particular schema mode.

properties.description: Degree to which the mode is present

properties.name: mode_frequency
properties.description: How often the mode is activated

edge_types.description: Indicates that a patient has a particular early maladaptive schema.
